rawText:
The popular image of science celebrates the moment of discovery — the flash of insight, the unexpected result, the eureka that overturns a long-held assumption — yet this narrative systematically undervalues the less glamorous but arguably more important process by which discoveries are confirmed, qualified, or quietly retired: replication.

Replication, in its most rigorous form, requires that an independent research team, using the same methods and materials described in the original study, produce results consistent with the original findings — a deceptively simple criterion that, in practice, exposes the fragility of a startling number of published claims.

The phrase "replication crisis" entered the mainstream vocabulary of science in the early 2010s, when a series of high-profile attempts to reproduce landmark findings in psychology, biomedicine, and economics revealed that a significant proportion of results — in some fields, more than half — could not be independently verified.

The roots of this crisis lie not in outright fraud, which remains statistically rare, but in a constellation of structural incentives that collectively push researchers toward practices that inflate the apparent significance of their results: p-hacking, in which analysts test multiple statistical comparisons until one crosses the threshold of significance; selective reporting, in which only the favorable outcomes of a study are disclosed; and publication bias, in which journals preferentially accept manuscripts that report positive, novel findings over those that report null results.

Statistical significance, conventionally defined as a p-value below 0.05, has been treated by many researchers as a binary verdict — significant or not — rather than as the probabilistic statement it actually is: that the observed result would occur fewer than five times in a hundred if the null hypothesis were true.

This categorical misinterpretation transforms a nuanced statistical tool into a blunt instrument of career advancement, because crossing the 0.05 threshold is often the difference between a publishable finding and a manuscript that no journal will accept, creating an incentive gradient that distorts the very evidence base on which policy decisions, medical treatments, and public understanding depend.

Effect size — the magnitude of the difference or relationship a study detects — is a far more informative metric than statistical significance alone, because a finding can be statistically significant yet practically negligible if the sample is large enough, a distinction that p-values by themselves cannot convey.

Pre-registration, in which researchers publicly commit to their hypotheses, methods, and analysis plans before collecting data, has emerged as one of the most promising structural remedies for the replication crisis, because it removes the possibility of post hoc rationalization — the practice of reframing unexpected results as though they had been predicted all along.

Null results — studies that find no statistically significant effect — are not failures but essential data points in the epistemic landscape, and the systematic suppression of null results through publication bias creates a distorted literature in which the apparent strength of an effect is an artifact of selective visibility rather than a genuine reflection of reality.

The deepest lesson of the replication crisis is not that science is broken but that the self-correcting mechanisms for which science is justly celebrated — peer review, replication, open data sharing — require institutional support, adequate funding, and a culture that rewards rigor as generously as it rewards novelty, conditions that the current incentive structures of academic publishing are poorly designed to provide.

overview.title: Why Replication Matters More Than Discovery

sentences.text: In science, finding something new is exciting, but checking if it is really true by doing the experiment again is even more important.
sentences.translation: 과학에서 새로운 것을 발견하는 것은 흥미롭지만, 실험을 다시 해서 정말 사실인지 확인하는 것이 더 중요해요.

sentences.text: Scientists discovered that many famous results could not be repeated when other people tried — this is called the "replication crisis."
sentences.translation: 과학자들은 다른 사람들이 시도했을 때 많은 유명한 결과가 반복될 수 없다는 것을 발견했는데, 이것을 "재현 위기"라고 해요.

sentences.text: Sometimes scientists accidentally find patterns in their data that are not really there, especially when they keep looking until something seems important.
sentences.translation: 때로는 과학자들이 실수로 실제로는 없는 데이터의 패턴을 찾는데, 특히 무언가 중요해 보일 때까지 계속 찾을 때 그래요.

sentences.text: Science journals prefer to publish exciting new discoveries instead of studies that checked older work, which creates a problem.
sentences.translation: 과학 저널은 이전 연구를 확인한 연구 대신 흥미로운 새 발견을 출판하는 것을 선호하는데, 이것이 문제를 만들어요.

sentences.text: One solution is for scientists to write down their plan before they start, so they cannot change it later to make their results look better.
sentences.translation: 한 가지 해결책은 과학자들이 시작하기 전에 계획을 적어두어 나중에 결과가 더 좋아 보이게 바꿀 수 없게 하는 거예요.

sentences.text: The replication crisis does not mean science is broken — it means science needs better rules to make sure its own checking system works properly.
sentences.translation: 재현 위기는 과학이 고장 났다는 뜻이 아니에요 — 과학의 자체 확인 시스템이 제대로 작동하도록 더 나은 규칙이 필요하다는 뜻이에요.

sentences.text: The article argues that science's popular narrative overemphasizes the moment of discovery while systematically undervaluing replication — the process of independently confirming results.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 과학의 대중적 서사가 발견의 순간을 지나치게 강조하면서 결과를 독립적으로 확인하는 과정인 재현을 체계적으로 과소평가한다고 주장해요.

sentences.text: The "replication crisis" is explained as the revelation, beginning in the 2010s, that a significant proportion of published findings in multiple fields could not be independently reproduced.
sentences.translation: "재현 위기"는 2010년대에 시작된, 여러 분야에서 출판된 발견의 상당 부분이 독립적으로 재현될 수 없다는 폭로로 설명돼요.

sentences.text: Root causes identified include p-hacking (testing multiple comparisons until one reaches significance), selective reporting, and publication bias toward positive results.
sentences.translation: 식별된 근본 원인에는 p-해킹(하나가 유의성에 도달할 때까지 여러 비교를 시험), 선택적 보고, 양성 결과에 대한 출판 편향이 포함돼요.

sentences.text: The article criticizes the treatment of statistical significance (p < 0.05) as a binary pass-fail verdict rather than the probabilistic statement it actually represents.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 통계적 유의성(p < 0.05)을 실제로 나타내는 확률적 진술이 아닌 이진적 합격-불합격 판정으로 취급하는 것을 비판해요.

sentences.text: Pre-registration — publicly committing to methods before collecting data — and effect size reporting are presented as key structural remedies for the crisis.
sentences.translation: 사전 등록 — 데이터 수집 전에 방법에 공개적으로 헌신 — 과 효과 크기 보고가 위기에 대한 핵심 구조적 치료법으로 제시돼요.

sentences.text: The conclusion frames the crisis not as evidence that science is broken but as evidence that its self-correcting mechanisms need better institutional support and funding.
sentences.translation: 결론은 위기를 과학이 고장 났다는 증거가 아니라 자기 교정 메커니즘이 더 나은 제도적 지원과 자금이 필요하다는 증거로 틀짓어요.

sentences.text: This essay performs a structural diagnosis of the replication crisis, locating its causes not in individual misconduct but in the incentive architecture of academic publishing — a system that rewards novelty, significance, and positive results at the systematic expense of confirmatory research.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 재현 위기에 대한 구조적 진단을 수행하며, 원인을 개인적 비행이 아닌 학술 출판의 인센티브 구조 — 확인 연구를 체계적으로 희생시키면서 참신성, 유의성, 양성 결과를 보상하는 시스템 — 에서 찾아요.

sentences.text: The "eureka narrative" in the opening sentence functions as a cultural diagnosis: the essay argues that the popular image of science as dramatic discovery actively impedes the institutional conditions necessary for reliable knowledge production.
sentences.translation: 첫 문장의 "유레카 서사"는 문화적 진단으로 기능해요: 에세이는 극적 발견으로서의 과학에 대한 대중적 이미지가 신뢰할 수 있는 지식 생산에 필요한 제도적 조건을 적극적으로 방해한다고 주장해요.

sentences.text: The taxonomy of structural incentives — p-hacking, selective reporting, publication bias — is presented not as a list of individual failings but as emergent properties of a system whose reward structure is misaligned with its epistemic mission.
sentences.translation: 구조적 인센티브의 분류법 — p-해킹, 선택적 보고, 출판 편향 — 은 개별적 결함의 목록이 아니라 보상 구조가 인식론적 사명과 정렬되지 않은 시스템의 창발적 속성으로 제시돼요.

sentences.text: The p-value critique performs a semiotic analysis of statistical notation: the essay shows how a continuous probability measure has been reified into a categorical gatekeeping device, transforming a tool of epistemic nuance into an instrument of career sorting.
sentences.translation: p-값 비판은 통계적 표기법에 대한 기호학적 분석을 수행해요: 에세이는 연속적 확률 측정이 범주적 문지기 장치로 물화되어, 인식론적 뉘앙스의 도구가 경력 분류의 도구로 변환되는 과정을 보여줘요.

sentences.text: Pre-registration is positioned as an epistemic technology — not merely a procedural reform but a structural intervention that disrupts the feedback loop between data collection and hypothesis formation that makes p-hacking possible.
sentences.translation: 사전 등록은 인식론적 기술로 위치되어요 — 단순한 절차적 개혁이 아니라 p-해킹을 가능하게 하는 데이터 수집과 가설 형성 사이의 피드백 루프를 교란하는 구조적 개입이에요.

sentences.text: The final sentence's "not that science is broken but that" construction performs the essay's central rhetorical move: reframing a narrative of failure as a narrative of institutional design, thereby converting despair into a reformist agenda.
sentences.translation: 마지막 문장의 "과학이 고장 난 것이 아니라" 구문은 에세이의 중심 수사적 전략을 수행해요: 실패의 서사를 제도 설계의 서사로 재구성하여 절망을 개혁주의적 의제로 전환해요.

sentences.original: The popular image of science celebrates the moment of discovery — the flash of insight, the unexpected result, the eureka that overturns a long-held assumption — yet this narrative systematically undervalues the less glamorous but arguably more important process by which discoveries are confirmed, qualified, or quietly retired: replication.
sentences.translation: 과학의 대중적 이미지는 발견의 순간을 찬양한다 — 통찰의 섬광, 예상치 못한 결과, 오래 유지된 가정을 뒤집는 유레카 — 그러나 이 서사는 발견이 확인되거나, 제한되거나, 조용히 폐기되는 덜 화려하지만 틀림없이 더 중요한 과정을 체계적으로 과소평가한다: 재현.

grammarElements.text: The popular image of science
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: celebrates the moment of discovery
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: — the flash of insight, the unexpected result, the eureka that overturns a long-held assumption —
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 3항 동격 병렬 (관계절)

grammarElements.text: yet this narrative systematically undervalues
grammarElements.label: yet 대조절

grammarElements.text: the less glamorous but arguably more important process by which discoveries are confirmed, qualified, or quietly retired
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (by which 관계절 + 3항 수동 병렬)

grammarElements.text: replication
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 동격 (핵심 용어 지연 공개)

vocabulary.example: The eureka moment is celebrated in popular culture.
Replication is less glamorous than discovery but more important.
Replication is the process by which findings are independently confirmed.

expressions.phrase: systematically undervalues
expressions.usage: "systematically"가 과소평가가 무작위적이 아닌 구조적임을 강조하는 비판적 부사

expressions.phrase: confirmed, qualified, or quietly retired
expressions.usage: 3항 수동 병렬이 재현의 세 가지 가능한 결과를 제시하며, "quietly retired"가 실패한 발견의 무명적 퇴장을 완곡하게 표현

patterns.pattern: The popular image of [domain] celebrates [dramatic moment] — [elaborating examples] — yet this narrative systematically undervalues [less visible but critical process]: [key term]
patterns.explanation: 대시 삽입 3항 병렬로 대중적 이미지를 구체화한 뒤, "yet"으로 과소평가된 과정을 대비하고, 콜론으로 핵심 용어를 지연 공개하는 문화 비판적 도입 패턴.
examples: The popular image of justice celebrates the moment of verdict — the dramatic cross-examination, the jury's pronouncement, the gavel that settles the case — yet this narrative systematically undervalues the less visible but arguably more consequential process by which legal precedents are established, refined, or silently abandoned: appellate review.

drillQuestions.question: "Eureka" refers to a moment of sudden _____.
"Systematically undervalues" means the neglect is _____.
"Quietly retired" means findings are _____ without announcement.
The colon before "replication" creates a moment of _____ revelation.

sentences.notes: 발견과 재현의 가치 비대칭을 비판하는 도입 문장. 대시 삽입의 3항 병렬이 발견의 화려함을 연출한 뒤, "yet"이 재현의 과소평가를 대비하고, 콜론 뒤 "replication"의 지연 공개가 핵심 주제를 극적으로 도입한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The popular image of science celebrates the moment of discovery
koreanAlignment.ko: 과학의 대중적 이미지는 발견의 순간을 찬양한다

koreanAlignment.en: the flash of insight, the unexpected result, the eureka that overturns a long-held assumption
koreanAlignment.ko: 통찰의 섬광, 예상치 못한 결과, 오래 유지된 가정을 뒤집는 유레카

koreanAlignment.en: yet this narrative systematically undervalues the less glamorous but arguably more important process
koreanAlignment.ko: 그러나 이 서사는 덜 화려하지만 틀림없이 더 중요한 과정을 체계적으로 과소평가한다

koreanAlignment.en: by which discoveries are confirmed, qualified, or quietly retired: replication
koreanAlignment.ko: 발견이 확인되거나, 제한되거나, 조용히 폐기되는: 재현

pronunciationNotes: eureka: /jʊˈriːkə/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "유리카"에 가깝다.
replication: /ˌreplɪˈkeɪʃən/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "레플리케이션"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Replication, in its most rigorous form, requires that an independent research team, using the same methods and materials described in the original study, produce results consistent with the original findings — a deceptively simple criterion that, in practice, exposes the fragility of a startling number of published claims.
sentences.translation: 가장 엄격한 형태의 재현은, 독립적인 연구팀이 원래 연구에 설명된 동일한 방법과 재료를 사용하여 원래 결과와 일치하는 결과를 산출할 것을 요구한다 — 실제로는 놀라운 수의 출판된 주장의 취약성을 노출시키는 기만적으로 단순한 기준이다.

grammarElements.text: Replication
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: in its most rigorous form
grammarElements.label: 삽입 전치사구 (조건)

grammarElements.text: requires that an independent research team ... produce results consistent with the original findings
grammarElements.label: 동사 + that절 (가정법 현재)

grammarElements.text: using the same methods and materials described in the original study
grammarElements.label: 현재분사구 (수단)

grammarElements.text: — a deceptively simple criterion that, in practice, exposes the fragility of a startling number of published claims
grammarElements.label: 대시 동격구 (관계절 + 삽입 부사구)

vocabulary.example: Rigorous replication requires identical methods and materials.
Replication exposes the fragility of many published claims.
The criterion is deceptively simple — harder than it looks.

expressions.phrase: in its most rigorous form
expressions.usage: 삽입구가 재현의 이상적 기준을 한정하여 현실과의 간극을 암시하는 학술적 한정 표현

expressions.phrase: a deceptively simple criterion
expressions.usage: "deceptively"가 표면적 단순성과 실제 난이도의 간극을 지적하는 역설적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Concept], in its most rigorous form, requires that [agent] [produce/achieve] [outcome] — a deceptively simple criterion that, in practice, exposes [unexpected difficulty/fragility]
patterns.explanation: 삽입구로 이상적 형태를 한정하고, "requires that" 가정법으로 기준을 정의한 뒤, "deceptively simple"로 표면과 실제의 간극을 드러내는 정의-역설 패턴.
examples: Informed consent, in its most rigorous form, requires that a patient understand the risks, alternatives, and implications of a proposed treatment — a deceptively simple criterion that, in practice, exposes the fragility of a remarkable number of clinical interactions.

drillQuestions.question: "In its most rigorous form" means the _____ version of replication.
"Deceptively simple" means it _____ simple but actually is not.
"Fragility" of published claims means they are _____ to replicate.
"Startling number" emphasizes the _____ high proportion of failures.

sentences.notes: 재현의 기준을 정의하면서 그 기만적 단순성을 드러내는 문장. "deceptively simple"이 핵심으로, 동일 방법으로 동일 결과를 얻는다는 단순한 기준이 실제로는 수많은 출판된 주장의 취약성을 폭로한다는 역설을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Replication, in its most rigorous form
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 엄격한 형태의 재현은

koreanAlignment.en: requires that an independent research team, using the same methods and materials
koreanAlignment.ko: 독립적인 연구팀이 동일한 방법과 재료를 사용하여

koreanAlignment.en: produce results consistent with the original findings
koreanAlignment.ko: 원래 결과와 일치하는 결과를 산출할 것을 요구한다

koreanAlignment.en: a deceptively simple criterion that, in practice, exposes the fragility of a startling number of published claims
koreanAlignment.ko: 실제로는 놀라운 수의 출판된 주장의 취약성을 노출시키는 기만적으로 단순한 기준이다

pronunciationNotes: rigorous: /ˈrɪɡərəs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "리거러스"에 가깝다.
deceptively: /dɪˈseptɪvli/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "디셉티블리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The phrase "replication crisis" entered the mainstream vocabulary of science in the early 2010s, when a series of high-profile attempts to reproduce landmark findings in psychology, biomedicine, and economics revealed that a significant proportion of results — in some fields, more than half — could not be independently verified.
sentences.translation: "재현 위기"라는 표현은 2010년대 초에 과학의 주류 어휘에 진입했는데, 심리학, 생의학, 경제학에서 획기적인 결과를 재현하려는 일련의 고위급 시도가 결과의 상당 부분이 — 일부 분야에서는 절반 이상이 — 독립적으로 검증될 수 없음을 드러냈을 때였다.

grammarElements.text: The phrase "replication crisis"
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: entered the mainstream vocabulary of science
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: in the early 2010s
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구

grammarElements.text: when a series of high-profile attempts to reproduce landmark findings in psychology, biomedicine, and economics
grammarElements.label: when 시간절 (3항 병렬)

grammarElements.text: revealed that a significant proportion of results — in some fields, more than half — could not be independently verified
grammarElements.label: 동사 + that절 (대시 삽입 수량 보충)

vocabulary.example: Landmark findings in psychology failed to replicate.
High-profile replication attempts drew public attention.
Many results could not be independently verified.

expressions.phrase: entered the mainstream vocabulary
expressions.usage: "entered the vocabulary"가 개념의 제도적 정착을 표현하는 비유적 표현

expressions.phrase: in some fields, more than half
expressions.usage: 대시 삽입으로 추가된 충격적 수치가 위기의 규모를 구체화하는 강화 표현

patterns.pattern: The phrase "[key term]" entered [mainstream discourse] in [time period], when [triggering events] revealed that [shocking proportion] — in some [contexts], [extreme figure] — could not be [expected outcome]
patterns.explanation: 용어의 등장 시점을 밝히고, when절로 촉발 사건을 제시한 뒤, 대시 삽입으로 충격적 수치를 추가하여 위기의 규모를 극적으로 전달하는 역사적 맥락 제공 패턴.
examples: The phrase "information bubble" entered the mainstream vocabulary of media criticism in the mid-2010s, when a series of unexpected electoral outcomes revealed that a significant proportion of voters — in some demographics, the majority — were consuming news from sources that never challenged their existing beliefs.

drillQuestions.question: "Entered the mainstream vocabulary" means the term became widely _____.
"Landmark findings" are results considered _____ in their field.
"More than half" could not be verified — this is a _____ proportion.
"Independently verified" means confirmed by _____ researchers.

sentences.notes: "재현 위기"라는 용어의 역사적 맥락을 제공하는 문장. "in some fields, more than half"의 대시 삽입이 위기의 규모를 충격적으로 구체화하고, 3항 분야 병렬(심리학, 생의학, 경제학)이 위기의 학제간 범위를 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: The phrase "replication crisis" entered the mainstream vocabulary of science in the early 2010s
koreanAlignment.ko: "재현 위기"라는 표현은 2010년대 초에 과학의 주류 어휘에 진입했다

koreanAlignment.en: when a series of high-profile attempts to reproduce landmark findings in psychology, biomedicine, and economics
koreanAlignment.ko: 심리학, 생의학, 경제학에서 획기적인 결과를 재현하려는 일련의 고위급 시도가

koreanAlignment.en: revealed that a significant proportion of results
koreanAlignment.ko: 결과의 상당 부분이

koreanAlignment.en: in some fields, more than half
koreanAlignment.ko: 일부 분야에서는 절반 이상이

koreanAlignment.en: could not be independently verified
koreanAlignment.ko: 독립적으로 검증될 수 없음을 드러냈을 때

pronunciationNotes: biomedicine: /ˌbaɪoʊˈmedɪsɪn/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "바이오메디신"에 가깝다.
verified: /ˈverɪfaɪd/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "베리파이드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The roots of this crisis lie not in outright fraud, which remains statistically rare, but in a constellation of structural incentives that collectively push researchers toward practices that inflate the apparent significance of their results: p-hacking, in which analysts test multiple statistical comparisons until one crosses the threshold of significance; selective reporting, in which only the favorable outcomes of a study are disclosed; and publication bias, in which journals preferentially accept manuscripts that report positive, novel findings over those that report null results.
sentences.translation: 이 위기의 뿌리는 통계적으로 드문 채로 남아 있는 노골적 사기가 아니라, 연구자를 결과의 겉보기 유의성을 부풀리는 관행으로 집단적으로 밀어붙이는 구조적 인센티브의 성좌에 있다: p-해킹, 분석가가 하나가 유의성의 임계치를 넘을 때까지 여러 통계적 비교를 시험하는 것; 선택적 보고, 연구의 유리한 결과만 공개되는 것; 그리고 출판 편향, 저널이 영 결과를 보고하는 것보다 양성적이고 새로운 결과를 보고하는 원고를 우선적으로 수락하는 것.

grammarElements.text: The roots of this crisis
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: lie not in outright fraud, which remains statistically rare
grammarElements.label: 동사 + not in (비제한 관계절)

grammarElements.text: but in a constellation of structural incentives that collectively push researchers toward practices that inflate the apparent significance of their results
grammarElements.label: but in 보어 (이중 관계절)

grammarElements.text: p-hacking, in which analysts test multiple statistical comparisons until one crosses the threshold of significance
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 첫째 항목 (정의적 관계절)

grammarElements.text: selective reporting, in which only the favorable outcomes of a study are disclosed
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 둘째 항목

grammarElements.text: and publication bias, in which journals preferentially accept manuscripts that report positive, novel findings over those that report null results
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 셋째 항목

vocabulary.example: P-hacking involves testing multiple comparisons to find significant results.
A constellation of structural incentives drives the replication crisis.
These practices inflate the apparent significance of results.

expressions.phrase: lie not in outright fraud ... but in a constellation of structural incentives
expressions.meaning: 노골적 사기가 아니라 구조적 인센티브의 성좌에 있다
expressions.usage: "not A but B"가 위기의 원인을 개인적 비행에서 구조적 인센티브로 전환하는 핵심 재프레이밍 표현

expressions.phrase: crosses the threshold of significance
expressions.usage: 통계적 유의성을 물리적 경계 넘기로 비유하여 p-해킹의 목표를 가시화하는 표현

patterns.pattern: The roots of [crisis] lie not in [individual cause], which remains [rare], but in a constellation of [structural causes] that collectively [push/drive toward]: [cause 1], in which [definition]; [cause 2], in which [definition]; and [cause 3], in which [definition]
patterns.explanation: "not A but B"로 원인을 개인에서 구조로 전환하고, 콜론 + 세미콜론으로 세 가지 구조적 원인을 각각 "in which" 관계절로 정의하는 구조적 원인 분석 패턴.
examples: The roots of this educational inequality lie not in individual teacher incompetence, which remains the exception, but in a constellation of structural disadvantages that collectively deprive students of opportunity: underfunding, in which schools in low-income districts receive fewer resources per pupil; tracking, in which students are sorted into ability groups that reinforce existing disparities; and curriculum standardization, in which a one-size-fits-all approach penalizes students whose learning styles differ from the norm.

drillQuestions.question: "P-hacking" means testing _____ comparisons until one is significant.
"Constellation" is used metaphorically to mean a _____ of factors.
"Outright fraud" is described as statistically _____.
"Publication bias" favors _____ results over null results.

sentences.notes: 위기의 구조적 원인을 체계적으로 분석하는 글의 핵심 문장. "not in fraud but in structural incentives"가 원인의 재프레이밍을 수행하고, 콜론 뒤 3항 세미콜론 나열이 p-해킹, 선택적 보고, 출판 편향을 각각 정의한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The roots of this crisis lie not in outright fraud, which remains statistically rare
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 위기의 뿌리는 통계적으로 드문 노골적 사기가 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but in a constellation of structural incentives that collectively push researchers toward practices that inflate the apparent significance
koreanAlignment.ko: 결과의 겉보기 유의성을 부풀리는 관행으로 밀어붙이는 구조적 인센티브의 성좌에 있다

koreanAlignment.en: p-hacking, in which analysts test multiple statistical comparisons until one crosses the threshold of significance
koreanAlignment.ko: p-해킹, 하나가 유의성의 임계치를 넘을 때까지 여러 통계적 비교를 시험하는 것

koreanAlignment.en: selective reporting, in which only the favorable outcomes are disclosed
koreanAlignment.ko: 선택적 보고, 유리한 결과만 공개되는 것

koreanAlignment.en: and publication bias, in which journals preferentially accept positive, novel findings over null results
koreanAlignment.ko: 출판 편향, 양성적이고 새로운 결과를 영 결과보다 우선 수락하는 것

pronunciationNotes: constellation: /ˌkɑːnstəˈleɪʃən/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "칸스텔레이션"에 가깝다.
inflate: /ɪnˈfleɪt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인플레이트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Statistical significance, conventionally defined as a p-value below 0.05, has been treated by many researchers as a binary verdict — significant or not — rather than as the probabilistic statement it actually is: that the observed result would occur fewer than five times in a hundred if the null hypothesis were true.
sentences.translation: 통계적 유의성은 관습적으로 p-값 0.05 미만으로 정의되며, 많은 연구자에 의해 실제로 그것이 나타내는 확률적 진술 — 영 가설이 참일 경우 관찰된 결과가 100번 중 5번 미만으로 발생할 것이라는 — 이 아니라 이진적 판정 — 유의하거나 아니거나 — 으로 취급되어 왔다.

grammarElements.text: Statistical significance
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: conventionally defined as a p-value below 0.05
grammarElements.label: 축약 분사구 (관습적 정의)

grammarElements.text: has been treated by many researchers as a binary verdict — significant or not
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재완료 수동 + as + 대시 삽입)

grammarElements.text: rather than as the probabilistic statement it actually is
grammarElements.label: rather than 대비구

grammarElements.text: that the observed result would occur fewer than five times in a hundred if the null hypothesis were true
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 that 동격절 (가정법 과거)

vocabulary.example: A p-value is a probabilistic statement, not a verdict.
Researchers wrongly treat significance as a binary verdict.
The p-value assumes the null hypothesis is true.

expressions.phrase: has been treated as a binary verdict rather than as
expressions.usage: "has been treated as A rather than as B"가 오해의 역사적 축적을 현재완료로 표현하고, "rather than"으로 올바른 해석을 대비

expressions.phrase: the probabilistic statement it actually is
expressions.usage: "it actually is"가 관계대명사 생략 + 강조 부사로, 오해와 실제의 간극을 강조하는 교정적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Technical concept], conventionally defined as [standard definition], has been treated by [practitioners] as [oversimplified interpretation] rather than as [accurate interpretation]: [precise explanation]
patterns.explanation: 축약 분사구로 관습적 정의를 밝히고, "has been treated as A rather than as B"로 오해를 교정한 뒤, 콜론으로 정확한 해석을 제시하는 기술적 오해 교정 패턴.
examples: Informed consent, conventionally defined as patient agreement to a medical procedure, has been treated by many practitioners as a legal formality — signed or not — rather than as the ongoing dialogic process it actually is: a continuous negotiation in which the patient's understanding evolves as new information becomes available.

drillQuestions.question: "P-value below 0.05" is the conventional threshold for statistical _____.
"Binary verdict" means treating it as simply _____ or not.
"Null hypothesis" is the hypothesis that there is _____ effect.
"Fewer than five times in a hundred" equals _____ percent.

sentences.notes: 통계적 유의성의 오해를 교정하는 핵심 문장. "binary verdict" vs. "probabilistic statement"의 대비가 핵심이며, 콜론 뒤의 가정법 과거("if the null hypothesis were true")가 p-값의 정확한 의미를 제공한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Statistical significance, conventionally defined as a p-value below 0.05
koreanAlignment.ko: 통계적 유의성은 관습적으로 p-값 0.05 미만으로 정의되며

koreanAlignment.en: has been treated by many researchers as a binary verdict — significant or not
koreanAlignment.ko: 많은 연구자에 의해 이진적 판정으로 취급되어 왔다

koreanAlignment.en: rather than as the probabilistic statement it actually is
koreanAlignment.ko: 실제로 그것이 나타내는 확률적 진술이 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: that the observed result would occur fewer than five times in a hundred if the null hypothesis were true
koreanAlignment.ko: 영 가설이 참일 경우 관찰된 결과가 100번 중 5번 미만으로 발생할 것이라는

pronunciationNotes: probabilistic: /ˌprɑːbəbɪˈlɪstɪk/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "프라버빌리스틱"에 가깝다.
hypothesis: /haɪˈpɑːθəsɪs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "하이파서시스"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: This categorical misinterpretation transforms a nuanced statistical tool into a blunt instrument of career advancement, because crossing the 0.05 threshold is often the difference between a publishable finding and a manuscript that no journal will accept, creating an incentive gradient that distorts the very evidence base on which policy decisions, medical treatments, and public understanding depend.
sentences.translation: 이 범주적 오해석은 미묘한 통계적 도구를 경력 발전의 무딘 도구로 변환시킨다, 0.05 임계치를 넘는 것이 출판 가능한 발견과 어떤 저널도 수락하지 않을 원고의 차이인 경우가 많기 때문이며, 이는 정책 결정, 의료 치료, 공적 이해가 의존하는 바로 그 증거 기반을 왜곡하는 인센티브 기울기를 만든다.

grammarElements.text: This categorical misinterpretation
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: transforms a nuanced statistical tool into a blunt instrument of career advancement
grammarElements.label: 동사 (transform A into B)

grammarElements.text: because crossing the 0.05 threshold is often the difference between a publishable finding and a manuscript that no journal will accept
grammarElements.label: because 이유절 (the difference between A and B)

grammarElements.text: creating an incentive gradient that distorts the very evidence base on which policy decisions, medical treatments, and public understanding depend
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (관계절 + on which + 3항 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The 0.05 threshold creates an incentive gradient toward inflated results.
The categorical misinterpretation reduces nuance to binary choice.
A nuanced tool has become a blunt instrument of career advancement.

expressions.phrase: transforms a nuanced statistical tool into a blunt instrument of career advancement
expressions.meaning: 미묘한 통계적 도구를 경력 발전의 무딘 도구로 변환시킨다
expressions.usage: "nuanced" vs. "blunt"의 대비가 p-값의 용도 변질을 날카롭게 표현하는 비유적 변환 표현

expressions.phrase: the very evidence base on which ... depend
expressions.usage: "the very"가 왜곡의 대상이 핵심적 기반임을 강조하고, "on which"로 의존 관계를 명시하는 위기 강조 표현

patterns.pattern: This [misinterpretation] transforms [nuanced tool] into [blunt instrument], because [threshold] is often the difference between [success] and [failure], creating [distorting force] that [corrupts] the very [foundation] on which [critical domains] depend
patterns.explanation: "transform A into B"로 도구의 변질을 밝히고, because절로 구조적 원인을 제시한 뒤, 분사구문으로 왜곡의 파급 범위를 3항 병렬로 확대하는 인과적 확대 패턴.
examples: This categorical reduction transforms a rich qualitative assessment into a crude ranking instrument, because scoring above a certain threshold is often the difference between admission and rejection, creating a preparation industry that distorts the very educational values on which meaningful learning, genuine curiosity, and intellectual growth depend.

drillQuestions.question: "Nuanced" tool means the tool is _____ and sensitive.
"Blunt instrument" is the opposite of _____.
"Incentive gradient" means pressure that _____ researchers toward certain behavior.
Policy decisions _____ on the evidence base that is being distorted.

sentences.notes: p-값의 범주적 오해석이 만드는 인센티브 왜곡과 그 파급 효과를 분석하는 문장. "nuanced tool → blunt instrument"의 변환이 핵심 비유이며, "the very evidence base on which ... depend"가 왜곡의 심각성을 정책, 의료, 공적 이해의 세 영역으로 확대한다.

koreanAlignment.en: This categorical misinterpretation transforms a nuanced statistical tool into a blunt instrument of career advancement
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 범주적 오해석은 미묘한 통계적 도구를 경력 발전의 무딘 도구로 변환시킨다

koreanAlignment.en: because crossing the 0.05 threshold is often the difference between a publishable finding and a manuscript that no journal will accept
koreanAlignment.ko: 0.05 임계치를 넘는 것이 출판 가능한 발견과 어떤 저널도 수락하지 않을 원고의 차이이기 때문이며

koreanAlignment.en: creating an incentive gradient that distorts the very evidence base
koreanAlignment.ko: 바로 그 증거 기반을 왜곡하는 인센티브 기울기를 만든다

koreanAlignment.en: on which policy decisions, medical treatments, and public understanding depend
koreanAlignment.ko: 정책 결정, 의료 치료, 공적 이해가 의존하는

pronunciationNotes: categorical: /ˌkætəˈɡɔːrɪkəl/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "캐터고리컬"에 가깝다.
gradient: /ˈɡreɪdiənt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "그레이디언트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Effect size — the magnitude of the difference or relationship a study detects — is a far more informative metric than statistical significance alone, because a finding can be statistically significant yet practically negligible if the sample is large enough, a distinction that p-values by themselves cannot convey.
sentences.translation: 효과 크기 — 연구가 감지하는 차이 또는 관계의 크기 — 는 통계적 유의성만으로는 훨씬 더 유익한 지표인데, 표본이 충분히 크면 결과가 통계적으로 유의하면서도 실질적으로 무시할 만할 수 있으며, 이 구별은 p-값 자체로는 전달할 수 없기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: Effect size
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: — the magnitude of the difference or relationship a study detects —
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구 (정의)

grammarElements.text: is a far more informative metric than statistical significance alone
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 비교급 보어 (than)

grammarElements.text: because a finding can be statistically significant yet practically negligible if the sample is large enough
grammarElements.label: because 이유절 (significant yet negligible 역설)

grammarElements.text: a distinction that p-values by themselves cannot convey
grammarElements.label: 동격구 (관계절)

vocabulary.example: Effect size measures the magnitude of a study's finding.
A statistically significant result can be practically negligible.
Effect size is a more informative metric than p-value alone.

expressions.phrase: statistically significant yet practically negligible
expressions.usage: "yet"이 통계적 유의성과 실질적 중요성의 분리를 역설적으로 보여주는 핵심 구별 표현

expressions.phrase: p-values by themselves cannot convey
expressions.usage: "by themselves"가 p-값의 한계를 명시하고, "cannot"이 그 한계의 절대성을 강조하는 비판적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Alternative metric] — [definition] — is a far more informative metric than [standard metric] alone, because [finding] can be [standard-qualified] yet [alternative-qualified] if [condition], a distinction that [standard metric] by [itself/themselves] cannot convey
patterns.explanation: 대시 삽입으로 대안 지표를 정의하고, 비교급으로 우월성을 밝힌 뒤, because절의 역설과 동격구의 한계 지적으로 표준 지표의 불충분성을 논증하는 대안 지표 옹호 패턴.
examples: Comprehension depth — the degree to which a student can apply and transfer knowledge to novel situations — is a far more informative measure than test scores alone, because a student can score highly yet understand superficially if the questions test only recall, a distinction that standardized assessments by themselves cannot capture.

drillQuestions.question: "Effect size" measures the _____ of a difference or relationship.
"Practically negligible" means unimportant in _____ terms.
A large sample can make a _____ effect statistically significant.
"By themselves" emphasizes that p-values are _____ alone.

sentences.notes: 효과 크기를 p-값보다 유익한 지표로 옹호하는 문장. "statistically significant yet practically negligible"의 역설이 핵심으로, 통계적 유의성과 실질적 중요성의 분리를 명확하게 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Effect size — the magnitude of the difference or relationship a study detects
koreanAlignment.ko: 효과 크기 — 연구가 감지하는 차이 또는 관계의 크기

koreanAlignment.en: is a far more informative metric than statistical significance alone
koreanAlignment.ko: 통계적 유의성만으로는 훨씬 더 유익한 지표이다

koreanAlignment.en: because a finding can be statistically significant yet practically negligible if the sample is large enough
koreanAlignment.ko: 표본이 충분히 크면 결과가 통계적으로 유의하면서도 실질적으로 무시할 만할 수 있기 때문이다

koreanAlignment.en: a distinction that p-values by themselves cannot convey
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 구별은 p-값 자체로는 전달할 수 없다

pronunciationNotes: negligible: /ˈneɡlɪdʒəbəl/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "네글리저블"에 가깝다.
magnitude: /ˈmæɡnɪtuːd/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "매그니튜드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Pre-registration, in which researchers publicly commit to their hypotheses, methods, and analysis plans before collecting data, has emerged as one of the most promising structural remedies for the replication crisis, because it removes the possibility of post hoc rationalization — the practice of reframing unexpected results as though they had been predicted all along.
sentences.translation: 사전 등록, 연구자가 데이터 수집 전에 가설, 방법, 분석 계획에 공개적으로 헌신하는 것은, 재현 위기에 대한 가장 유망한 구조적 치료법 중 하나로 부상했는데, 사후 합리화의 가능성 — 예상치 못한 결과를 마치 처음부터 예측한 것처럼 재구성하는 관행 — 을 제거하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: Pre-registration
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: in which researchers publicly commit to their hypotheses, methods, and analysis plans before collecting data
grammarElements.label: in which 비제한 관계절 (정의)

grammarElements.text: has emerged as one of the most promising structural remedies for the replication crisis
grammarElements.label: 동사 + as 보어 (최상급)

grammarElements.text: because it removes the possibility of post hoc rationalization
grammarElements.label: because 이유절

grammarElements.text: — the practice of reframing unexpected results as though they had been predicted all along
grammarElements.label: 대시 동격구 (as though 가정법)

vocabulary.example: Pre-registration commits researchers to their methods before data collection.
Post hoc rationalization makes unexpected results appear predicted.
Pre-registration is a structural remedy for the replication crisis.

expressions.phrase: has emerged as one of the most promising structural remedies
expressions.meaning: 가장 유망한 구조적 치료법 중 하나로 부상했다
expressions.usage: "has emerged as"가 점진적 인식 변화를 표현하고, "structural"이 개인적이 아닌 제도적 해결임을 강조

expressions.phrase: as though they had been predicted all along
expressions.usage: "as though + 대과거"가 사후 합리화의 허구적 성격을 문법적으로 드러내는 가정법 표현

patterns.pattern: [Reform], in which [how it works], has emerged as one of the most promising [structural remedies] for [crisis], because it removes the possibility of [harmful practice] — [definition of harmful practice]
patterns.explanation: 관계절로 개혁을 정의하고, "has emerged as"로 그 부상을 밝힌 뒤, because절과 대시 동격구로 개혁이 막는 구체적 관행을 설명하는 구조적 해결책 소개 패턴.
examples: Open-access publishing, in which articles are made freely available upon publication, has emerged as one of the most promising structural remedies for the knowledge inequality crisis, because it removes the possibility of paywall exclusion — the practice of restricting access to publicly funded research behind subscription fees that only wealthy institutions can afford.

drillQuestions.question: "Pre-registration" means committing to methods _____ collecting data.
"Post hoc rationalization" means explaining results _____ the fact.
"As though they had been predicted" uses the _____ tense.
"Structural remedy" addresses the _____ of the problem, not symptoms.

sentences.notes: 사전 등록을 구조적 해결책으로 소개하는 문장. "removes the possibility of post hoc rationalization"이 핵심으로, 대시 동격구의 "as though they had been predicted all along"이 사후 합리화의 허구적 성격을 가정법으로 생생하게 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Pre-registration, in which researchers publicly commit to their hypotheses, methods, and analysis plans before collecting data
koreanAlignment.ko: 사전 등록, 연구자가 데이터 수집 전에 가설, 방법, 분석 계획에 공개적으로 헌신하는 것은

koreanAlignment.en: has emerged as one of the most promising structural remedies for the replication crisis
koreanAlignment.ko: 재현 위기에 대한 가장 유망한 구조적 치료법 중 하나로 부상했다

koreanAlignment.en: because it removes the possibility of post hoc rationalization
koreanAlignment.ko: 사후 합리화의 가능성을 제거하기 때문이다

koreanAlignment.en: the practice of reframing unexpected results as though they had been predicted all along
koreanAlignment.ko: 예상치 못한 결과를 마치 처음부터 예측한 것처럼 재구성하는 관행

pronunciationNotes: pre-registration: /ˌpriːˌredʒɪˈstreɪʃən/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "프리레지스트레이션"에 가깝다.
rationalization: /ˌræʃənələˈzeɪʃən/ — 다섯 번째 음절에 강세. "래셔널리제이션"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Null results — studies that find no statistically significant effect — are not failures but essential data points in the epistemic landscape, and the systematic suppression of null results through publication bias creates a distorted literature in which the apparent strength of an effect is an artifact of selective visibility rather than a genuine reflection of reality.
sentences.translation: 영 결과 — 통계적으로 유의한 효과를 찾지 못하는 연구 — 는 실패가 아니라 인식론적 풍경의 필수적 데이터 포인트이며, 출판 편향을 통한 영 결과의 체계적 억압은 효과의 겉보기 강도가 현실의 진정한 반영이 아니라 선택적 가시성의 인공물인 왜곡된 문헌을 만든다.

grammarElements.text: Null results
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: — studies that find no statistically significant effect —
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구 (정의)

grammarElements.text: are not failures but essential data points in the epistemic landscape
grammarElements.label: 동사 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: and the systematic suppression of null results through publication bias
grammarElements.label: and 병렬 주어

grammarElements.text: creates a distorted literature in which the apparent strength of an effect is an artifact of selective visibility rather than a genuine reflection of reality
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어 (in which 관계절 + rather than)

vocabulary.example: Null results are suppressed by publication bias.
The apparent strength of an effect may be an artifact of selective visibility.
Systematic suppression of null results distorts the scientific literature.

expressions.phrase: not failures but essential data points
expressions.usage: "not A but B"가 영 결과에 대한 부정적 인식을 교정하고 인식론적 가치를 부여하는 핵심 재프레이밍 표현

expressions.phrase: an artifact of selective visibility rather than a genuine reflection of reality
expressions.meaning: 현실의 진정한 반영이 아니라 선택적 가시성의 인공물
expressions.usage: "rather than"이 왜곡의 본질을 명시하고, "artifact" vs. "genuine reflection"의 대비가 허구와 현실을 분리하는 비판적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Stigmatized outcome] — [definition] — are not [negative label] but [essential function], and the systematic [suppression/neglect] of [these outcomes] creates a distorted [domain] in which [apparent quality] is an artifact of [selection mechanism] rather than a genuine reflection of [reality]
patterns.explanation: "not A but B"로 오명을 교정하고, "and"로 억압의 결과를 밝힌 뒤, "in which ... artifact ... rather than"으로 왜곡의 메커니즘을 정밀하게 분석하는 오명 교정 + 왜곡 분석 패턴.
examples: Negative reviews — assessments that identify fundamental flaws in a product — are not hostile acts but essential quality signals in the marketplace, and the systematic removal of negative reviews through algorithmic filtering creates a distorted consumer landscape in which the apparent quality of a product is an artifact of suppressed criticism rather than a genuine reflection of user experience.

drillQuestions.question: "Null results" find _____ statistically significant effect.
"Artifact" here means a _____ product of selective visibility.
"Selective visibility" means only _____ results are visible.
Null results are reframed as _____ data points.

sentences.notes: 영 결과의 인식론적 가치를 재평가하고 출판 편향의 왜곡 효과를 분석하는 문장. "not failures but essential data points"가 영 결과에 대한 오명을 교정하고, "artifact of selective visibility"가 왜곡의 메커니즘을 정밀하게 명명한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Null results — studies that find no statistically significant effect
koreanAlignment.ko: 영 결과 — 통계적으로 유의한 효과를 찾지 못하는 연구

koreanAlignment.en: are not failures but essential data points in the epistemic landscape
koreanAlignment.ko: 실패가 아니라 인식론적 풍경의 필수적 데이터 포인트이며

koreanAlignment.en: the systematic suppression of null results through publication bias creates a distorted literature
koreanAlignment.ko: 출판 편향을 통한 영 결과의 체계적 억압은 왜곡된 문헌을 만든다

koreanAlignment.en: in which the apparent strength of an effect is an artifact of selective visibility rather than a genuine reflection of reality
koreanAlignment.ko: 효과의 겉보기 강도가 현실의 진정한 반영이 아니라 선택적 가시성의 인공물인

pronunciationNotes: artifact: /ˈɑːrtɪfækt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "아티팩트"에 가깝다.
suppression: /səˈpreʃən/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "서프레션"에 가깝다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 오명 교정 및 왜곡 분석 (stigma correction and distortion analysis)

sentences.original: The deepest lesson of the replication crisis is not that science is broken but that the self-correcting mechanisms for which science is justly celebrated — peer review, replication, open data sharing — require institutional support, adequate funding, and a culture that rewards rigor as generously as it rewards novelty, conditions that the current incentive structures of academic publishing are poorly designed to provide.
sentences.translation: 재현 위기의 가장 깊은 교훈은 과학이 고장 났다는 것이 아니라, 과학이 정당하게 찬양받는 자기 교정 메커니즘 — 동료 심사, 재현, 공개 데이터 공유 — 이 제도적 지원, 적절한 자금, 그리고 참신성만큼이나 후하게 엄격함을 보상하는 문화를 필요로 한다는 것이며, 이는 현재 학술 출판의 인센티브 구조가 제공하도록 잘못 설계된 조건들이다.

grammarElements.text: The deepest lesson of the replication crisis
grammarElements.label: 주어 (최상급)

grammarElements.text: is not that science is broken
grammarElements.label: 동사 + not that절

grammarElements.text: but that the self-correcting mechanisms for which science is justly celebrated
grammarElements.label: but that절 (for which 관계절)

grammarElements.text: — peer review, replication, open data sharing —
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 3항 열거

grammarElements.text: require institutional support, adequate funding, and a culture that rewards rigor as generously as it rewards novelty
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 3항 목적어 (as ... as 비교)

grammarElements.text: conditions that the current incentive structures of academic publishing are poorly designed to provide
grammarElements.label: 동격구 (관계절)

vocabulary.example: Science's self-correcting mechanisms require institutional support.
The culture should reward rigor as generously as novelty.
Current incentive structures are poorly designed to support replication.

expressions.phrase: not that science is broken but that
expressions.usage: "not A but B"가 절망적 해석을 교정하고 구조적 개혁의 가능성을 열어주는 핵심 재프레이밍 결론 표현

expressions.phrase: rewards rigor as generously as it rewards novelty
expressions.usage: "as generously as"가 현재의 불균형을 암시하면서 이상적 균형을 제시하는 비교적 처방 표현

patterns.pattern: The deepest lesson of [crisis] is not that [system] is broken but that [valued mechanisms] — [enumeration] — require [necessary conditions], conditions that [current structures] are poorly designed to provide
patterns.explanation: "not A but B"로 파괴적 해석을 교정하고, 대시 삽입으로 메커니즘을 열거한 뒤, "conditions that ... poorly designed to provide"로 구조적 개혁의 필요성을 밝히는 결론적 재프레이밍 패턴.
examples: The deepest lesson of the burnout epidemic is not that workers are too weak to cope but that the self-sustaining capacities for which human resilience is justly admired — rest, reflection, meaningful connection — require organizational support, adequate boundaries, and a culture that rewards sustainability as generously as it rewards productivity, conditions that the current design of knowledge-economy workplaces is poorly equipped to provide.

drillQuestions.question: "Not that science is broken" _____ a despair narrative.
"Self-correcting mechanisms" include peer review, replication, and _____.
"Rewards rigor as generously as novelty" implies the current system rewards novelty _____.
"Poorly designed to provide" means the current system _____ provide these conditions.

sentences.notes: 글의 결론으로, 재현 위기의 "가장 깊은 교훈"을 제시하는 문장. "not that science is broken but that"이 파괴적 해석을 교정하고, "rewards rigor as generously as it rewards novelty"가 이상적 문화를 비교급으로 제시하며, "poorly designed to provide"가 현재 구조의 부적합성을 결론으로 밝힌다.

koreanAlignment.en: The deepest lesson of the replication crisis is not that science is broken
koreanAlignment.ko: 재현 위기의 가장 깊은 교훈은 과학이 고장 났다는 것이 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but that the self-correcting mechanisms for which science is justly celebrated
koreanAlignment.ko: 과학이 정당하게 찬양받는 자기 교정 메커니즘이

koreanAlignment.en: peer review, replication, open data sharing
koreanAlignment.ko: 동료 심사, 재현, 공개 데이터 공유

koreanAlignment.en: require institutional support, adequate funding, and a culture that rewards rigor as generously as it rewards novelty
koreanAlignment.ko: 제도적 지원, 적절한 자금, 참신성만큼이나 후하게 엄격함을 보상하는 문화를 필요로 한다는 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: conditions that the current incentive structures of academic publishing are poorly designed to provide
koreanAlignment.ko: 현재 학술 출판의 인센티브 구조가 제공하도록 잘못 설계된 조건들이다

pronunciationNotes: rigor: /ˈrɪɡər/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "리거"에 가깝다.
adequately: /ˈædɪkwətli/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "애디퀴틀리"에 가깝다.
